description: User uploads test-resume.pdf and verifies it appears in the documents list

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9. Wait for the dashboard to load.
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/documents. Note the exact number of documents currently listed (could be 0 or more).
Click the Upload button or file upload CTA on the documents page. A file input dialog or upload form should appear.

Upload the file at path './fixtures/test-resume.pdf' (relative to the test runner working directory). If a file chooser dialog is triggered by Playwright, use it to select that file. Submit / confirm the upload.

Wait up to 10 seconds for the upload to complete. Verify: does a document named 'test-resume' or 'test-resume.pdf' now appear in the documents list? The count should have increased by 1 from what you noted in step 2.

Check for error messages or console errors. If the upload succeeded and the document appears: find and delete the just-uploaded 'test-resume.pdf' to clean up test data; confirm it is removed. PASS if the full upload-verify-delete cycle works. FAIL if the upload button is missing, the file is rejected, or the document does not appear after upload. Screenshot.

description: User adds a job portal scanning config and then deletes it — full create/delete lifecycle

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/portals
Click the '+ Add Portal' button to open the add portal form
Fill in the form: portal_name = 'qa-test-portal', search_keywords = 'software engineer', preferred_locations = 'Bangalore', set scan_time to any available option. Submit the form.
Verify: the form submits without a 500 error. Does the new portal 'qa-test-portal' appear in the portals list?

If the portal was created: find the delete button for 'qa-test-portal' and delete it. Confirm the deletion and verify it is removed from the list. PASS if the full create→list→delete lifecycle works. Report any errors seen and take a screenshot.

description: Portrait page shows correct state: locked screen with instructions OR portrait card with share section

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/portrait

Check state A - Locked screen: if no portrait exists, is there a locked screen shown with a clear message explaining what the user needs to do to unlock their portrait (e.g. upload a resume)? Is the message helpful or cryptic?
Check state B - Portrait exists: if a portrait is shown, does it have quadrants or sections with real content? Is there a Regenerate button?
Scroll to the bottom of the page. Is there a Share Profile or Create share link section? If yes, click the create share link button and verify a shareable URL is generated.
Check browser console for JS errors and network tab for 404/500 calls. Report: which state is shown (locked or portrait)? Does it make sense to a new user? Is the share section present? Screenshot.

description: Applications page shows correct empty state or real applications, with meaningful CTA

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/applications
Verify the page loads without a 500 error or blank screen

Check the page content regardless of state. If empty: verify the empty state has a helpful explanation (explaining that applications appear after job scanning) AND a CTA link pointing to /jp/portals or /jp/jobs. If applications are listed: verify each row shows job title, company, status, and date.

If applications are listed: click on the first entry. Verify a detail view, modal, or expanded row appears. PASS if detail opens. PASS also if no applications exist (empty state verified in step 4). FAIL only if applications exist but clicking them does nothing.
Check browser console for JS errors. Report: describe what is shown (empty state or application list). Screenshot.

description: Scan history page loads and shows history or a meaningful empty state

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/scan-history
Verify the page loads without a 500 error or blank screen
If no scan history: is there a clear empty state? Does it tell the user scans happen automatically once a portal is configured?
If scan history exists: do entries show portal name, scan time, jobs found count, and status? Are there any entries with errors?
Report: describe what is shown. Screenshot.

description: JP configuration settings save and persist after navigation — form is not broken

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/config
Describe what configuration options are visible (notification preferences, scan settings, alert thresholds, etc.)
Try to change one setting (any toggle or input that is clearly safe to change). Click Save if there is a save button.
Navigate away to /jp/dashboard, then navigate back to /jp/config. Did the setting persist?

Check browser console for JS errors on save and confirm the network save request returned 200. Report: which settings are available, did the save/persist cycle work, and were there any broken inputs, missing labels, or uncaught errors? Screenshot.

description: Subscription page shows current tier correctly and upgrade options are visible

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/subscription
Verify: is the current tier shown? It should be Standard for this test account.
Are upgrade options visible? If higher tier exists, are the features listed clearly?
Is there a Manage Billing or payment link? Does it go somewhere sensible and not a 404?
Report: describe the subscription page. Does tier display match what was granted? Screenshot.

description: Cross-sell banner suppression is correct for a user who already has Content Companion access — and CC nav entry is present

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9. This account has BOTH job-portal (standard) AND content-companion (free) grants.

Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/dashboard and wait for the page to load. Even after clearing localStorage (execute in browser console: localStorage.removeItem('ccCrossSellDismissed')), the cross-sell banner should NOT appear — because the user already has CC access.

Verify: there is NO banner on the dashboard mentioning 'Adding a chatbot to your website?' or any 'Try Content Companion' prompt. The absence of this banner is the CORRECT behaviour for a user who already has a CC grant. FAIL only if such a banner IS shown to a user who already has CC access.

Verify that the portal sidebar or header contains a navigation entry for Content Companion (e.g. a 'Content Companion' link, a CC product switcher, or similar). A user with CC access should be able to navigate to CC directly, not be cross-sold it.
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/cc — the Content Companion section. Verify it loads without a 403 or 'Access Denied' error. The user holds a CC grant so this should be accessible.
Report: was the cross-sell banner correctly absent? Was CC navigation reachable? Any JS errors? Screenshot the dashboard showing the absence of the banner and the presence of CC navigation.

description: Jobs list shows real jobs or meaningful empty state; job detail view works if jobs exist

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/jobs
If jobs are listed: click on one job. Does a detail view or modal open? Does it show job title, company, description, and an apply option?
If no jobs: note the empty state message. Is it clear that jobs appear after scan runs? Is there a link to configure portals?
Check: is there a search or filter capability on the jobs list? If yes, try filtering by a keyword and report if it works.
Report: how many jobs are shown? Does the list render well? Any broken pagination or infinite scroll? Screenshot.

description: New user setup guide is shown on dashboard and guides correctly through onboarding — first-time user experience

steps.description: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-test-journeyhawk@phronex.com / HMqt4kH5loXkeAtvQSndQ7zNmow!Phx#9
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/dashboard
Look for a setup guide or onboarding banner (something like 'New to Job Portal? Follow the setup guide...'). Is it visible?
If visible: click 'Get Started' or the CTA on the banner. Where does it navigate to? Does the linked page load correctly and does it make sense as a first step?
Is there a Help guide accessible via a Help button or sidebar? Click it and report: does it open a guide panel? Does the content make sense for a new JP user?
Report: describe the onboarding experience. Would a real new user know what to do first? Is the journey from signup to first scan clear? Screenshot.
